PLAINTIFF ADMITS THAT SHE SIGNED THE CONSENT OF SPOUSES TO THE SHAREHOLDERS AGREEMENT. IN THAT AGREEMENT, PLAINTIFF AGREED TO ABIDE BY THE TERMS OF THE SHAREHOLDERS AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF CANNOT AVOID THE AGREEMENT BASED ON THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS BECAUSE SHE ADMITS THAT SHE DID NOT READ THE AGREEMENT PRIOR TO SIGNING IT. (ROSENTHAL V. GREAT WESTERN FIN. SECURITIES CORP. (1996) 14 CAL.4TH 394, 423.) DEFENDANTS' REQUEST TO PRESENT ORAL TESTIMONY IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Sep-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, David M. Furbush of the law offices of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP (650-233-4623) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorneys, Brian Ketroser and Bahram Seyedin-Noor of the law offices of Alto Litigation (415-830-5897) appear as counsels for defendants/opposing parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF STEPHANIE STEVENS' Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award is DENIED. Ms. Stevens failed to show any of the limited grounds for the vacation of an arbitration award per CCP 1286.2. The Arbitrator did violate CCP 1283.4 by failing to explicitly address Ms. Stevens' argument that the Shareholders Agreement was unenforceable because it was not signed by all shareholders. By ruling against Ms. Stevens and more specifically ruling that the Shareholders Agreement was enforceable, the Arbitrator necessarily resolved Ms. Stevens' argument regarding the lack of a shareholder signature adverse to Ms. Stevens. Neither CCP 1283.4 nor any case law regarding arbitration awards requires more. Nor did Ms. Stevens show that the arbitration testimony by defendant Ridge Sampson was false, much less that it resulted in the arbitration award being procured by fraud. There is no indication in the record on this petition that the Arbitrator would have reached a different result even if it had been shown that Mr. Sampson's testimony about an exit strategy was untrue. The exit strategy testimony is not directly pertinent to the Arbitrator's primary determination that Ms. Stevens was not entitled to the fair market value of the company. Ms. Stevens failed to respond to defendants' request for an award of fees and costs of $27,735.00 and thus defendants are awarded that amount as the prevailing parties in this court per section 32 of the Shareholders Agreement. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, e-mail: geales@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Off Calendar Mar-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RIDGE SAMPSON, STEPHANIE GREIN, NANCY GIROUARD, RANDALL DRULLINGER, GEOFF SPENCER, NEALESH PATEL, SEAN SHOFFSTALL AND SYCLE, INC'S motion to compel arbitration and for stay this case pending completion of the arbitration is granted. Plaintiff admits that she signed the Consent of Spouses to the Shareholders Agreement. In that agreement, plaintiff agreed to abide by the terms of the Shareholders Agreement. One of those terms was for binding arbitration. Plaintiff cannot avoid the agreement based on the alleged misrepresentations because she admits that she did not read the agreement prior to signing it. (Rosenthal v. Great Western Fin. Securities Corp. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 394, 423.) Defendants' request to present oral testimony is denied. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
